title: Baked backslash loader paths break LoRA workflows on Linux/macOS LOCAL engine (portable builds)
description: Found 2026-07-05 during MPI-197 stock-pod test: workflow templates bake Windows backslash loader values (e.g. Merged-Loras lora_name 'ltx-2.3\LTX23_softenhance_abliterated_detailer_merged.safetensors'). comfyController.js step 3b heals '\'->'/' ONLY when remoteEngineClient.isRemote() (MPI-141). LOCAL engine on Linux/macOS portable builds gets the raw backslash value -> ComfyUI enumerates with '/' -> value_not_in_list 400 on every LoRA-baked workflow. The 'flipping would BREAK local' comment is Windows-local-only truth. FIX: gate the PATH_INPUTS heal on engine OS, not remote-ness — normalize when remote OR local platform != win32 (renderer: get platform from existing server/preload channel, don't assume process.platform available). Sweep: same baked-value risk for upscale_model/vae_name/clip_name etc (PATH_INPUTS list already covers). Verify on a Linux portable build (or CI) with LTX_t2v LoRA workflow before next distribution release. Related: MPI-141, docs/add-model-playbook.md loader-path normalization trap, .claude/rules/comfy_engine.md § list-files engine separator.

attention.reason: Moved back to To do; reconcile active work state.
